symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse, poorly localised bone pain (lower back, pelvis, legs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse skeletal pain from undermineralised osteoid is characteristic of osteomalacia.
Bone pain is non-specific and keeps myeloma on the differential until screened.
Diffuse, non-dermatomal pain argues against a focal radiculopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical proximal muscle weakness / myopathy (difficulty rising from a chair, waddling gait)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proximal myopathy is a well-recognised feature of vitamin D deficiency/osteomalacia.
Objective proximal weakness points to organic disease rather than a functional pain syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bone tenderness on firm palpation of the sternum and tibiae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal bony tenderness over the sternum and shins is typical of osteomalacia — but requires hands-on examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: New severe cramping/spasm of calves and hands (neuromuscular irritability)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cramp/spasm reflects increased neuromuscular excitability from a falling ionised calcium.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Chvostek's sign (facial twitch on tapping the facial nerve)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Latent tetany sign of hypocalcaemia — requires a clinician-performed examination, so unavailable via telehealth.
Lack of cutaneous UVB exposure is the dominant mechanism for severe vitamin D deficiency.

investigation_details.expected_result: Severely low (deficient) — anticipated, pending laboratory confirmation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without the level, deficiency cannot be confirmed or graded and replacement cannot be dosed safely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A severely low 25-OH vitamin D confirms deficiency — a laboratory test never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum calcium / phosphate / PTH / ALP profile

investigation_details.investigation_name: Corrected calcium, phosphate, PTH, alkaline phosphatase
investigation_details.expected_result: Low/low-normal calcium and phosphate with elevated PTH and ALP (secondary hyperparathyroidism) — anticipated, pending laboratory confirmation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Symptomatic hypocalcaemia cannot be graded and secondary hyperparathyroidism cannot be identified without these.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Corrected/ionised calcium is required to confirm and grade the hypocalcaemia driving the cramps — unobtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe vitamin D deficiency with osteomalacia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Diffuse bone pain + proximal muscle weakness + bone tenderness + low 25-OH vitamin D together are strongly suggestive of osteomalacia.
Cramps/spasm with a positive Chvostek's sign indicate latent tetany from a falling calcium — the acute, escalation-relevant thread of the case.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades deficiency; required before dosing replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Corrected calcium, phosphate, PTH, alkaline phosphatase
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Grades the hypocalcaemia driving the cramps and identifies secondary hyperparathyroidism.
Screens for multiple myeloma as a not-to-miss cause of bone pain, as flagged in the source assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Renal function and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the metabolic workup and myeloma screen and informs safe replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate ambulance / ECG for isolated cramps
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: In the absence of sustained tetany, laryngospasm or seizure, an emergency ambulance is over-triage; urgent same-day bloods and in-person review are the correct level.
